channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Deep pothole (roughly 7 inches deep) On the crossing/speedbump outside Woodknoll Drive, 15th March
item.description:
This pothole has been there for a few weeks from what i’ve noticed as i don’t drive down the road often. But It’s a relatively blind corner and you wouldn’t expect there to be a pothole that side on the side of a speedbump. It seems dangerous as it’s also a crossing and could cause harm to anyone partially sighted who may not see the pothole.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Long stretch of potholes from Perry Road leading into Nazeing., 15th March
item.description:
Far too dangerous to stop and photograph as so many on top of each other they all filled with water so are extremely dangerous. People have put cones as so many tired been damaged. Holes before and after were fixed few weeks back but work men ignored these ones and told residence they only do ones reported.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping, 15th March
item.description:
I have reported these items several times. Your operatives just remove some items and leave the rest to pile up again. It is unacceptable for us to live these items. I will be withholding my council charges payment if these items are not remove. I don't understand why I have to keep reporting the dumping of rubbish whilst your ground maintenance ignore it for weeks.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kidd Place, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars park throughout the day on double red lines - no enforcement, 15th March
item.description:
This is an ongoing problem on Mission Place. Cars park or remain idle on double red lines throughout the day. The problem is being aggravated with the opening of a night club nearby.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Mission Place, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light faulty for months, 15th March
item.description:
The street is very dark around this corner and the street light has been faulty for close to a year. Please can you fit X it as young people often meet around there on a night out and it's as dark as it gets.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pickhurst Park, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The front garden boundary between 19 and 20 Leighton Crescent, 15th March
item.description:
There are two overgrown trees. One (a bay tree) is impeding street lighting and safe pavement access. The issue has been present for a couple of years.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 71 A,B, Falkland Road, Tufnell Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light post not working, 14th March
item.description:
Light not working and it’s dark at night

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crossway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unknown fridge left outside, 14th March
item.description:
There is this fridge left in the pavement. Seems like the neighbours is not owning this. This has been here since I moved but I confirmed with the agent this is not from the property I bought. This is in between 37 and 38.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Surlingham Close, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light out, 14th March
item.description:
One not working one is dim
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stadium Road, Charlton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crossway, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish, 14th March
item.description:
Outside 36-44 Sutcliffe rd items of rubbish left
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sutcliffe Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Un safe parking on highway road, 14th March
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to enquire whether it would be possible to purchase, lease, or formally secure the parking space located directly in front of our property at Oaklands, Sewardstone Road, E4 7RG. At present, the space is frequently used by random vehicles (attached of the vehicle who parks 90% of the time) and this has begun to cause ongoing safety concerns for our household. We have experienced issues including blocked visibility when exiting our driveway, obstruction of access, and uncertainty regarding who is parking there. This situation has made us feel increasingly unsafe and concerned, particularly given the unpredictability of the vehicles using the space. We would therefore like to ask whether the council offers any scheme that would allow residents to purchase, lease, or reserve the parking space directly outside their home, or whether there are alternative measures that could be implemented to improve safety (for example, permit restrictions, residential designation, or protective markings). We are more than willing to pay any required fees if such an option is available. I would be grateful if you could advise on the process, eligibility criteria, and any next steps. This car has also started parking multiple different cars on both sides of the road. Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to your response. Kind regards, Nur Kurklu

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven paving slab on Crofton Avenue, 14th March
item.description:
Uneven paving slab outside number 2 Crofton Avenue, DA5. Observed for the first time today. It looks like new paving slabs have been laid very recently, resulting in the uneven surface. Concerned about the potential for someone to trip over this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Crofton Avenue, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 14th March
item.description:
By parish mews parish lane near bus stop
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parish Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 10 inch pothole next to the road drain., 14th March
item.description:
The area adjacent to the drain is breaking up.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beverley Road, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damage to grass verge by builders, 14th March
item.description:
No 76 is having renovation works and the builders are constantly driving on the grass verge and have now started to destroy the verge with it dug up and turning into a mud bank. Please rectify and also install posts. Can you please confirm actions you will take and this should be recharged to 76 and not charged to council tax payers. This is the 3rd such damage of this nature on Eden Park Ave over the last year

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 76, Eden Park Avenue, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Temporary traffic lights, 14th March
item.description:
The temporary traffic lights cannot be seen from the stop line causing cars to remain at the lights on green and constant road rage. Please either relocate the lights, introduce secondary lights, or relocate the stop line.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Church Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet
